'''Paloma D'asima''' was one of the [[Eleven Elders of the People|Eleven Elders]] of [[Emberlene]] at the time of the [[Caamas Document]] crisis.

==Biography==
On [[Karoly D'ulin]]'s recommendation, D'asima met with [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Vilim Disra|Disra]] and [[Grodin Tierce]] to discuss a [[Mistryl Shadow Guard]]/[[Imperial Remnant]] alliance on [[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]]. The con-artist [[Flim (Human)|Flim]], disguised as [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], was also involved in the negotiations, providing the appearance of confident leadership necessary to make Paloma consider the otherwise unthinkable alliance. Disra and Tierce were aware that [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]] planned an information raid on the [[Ubiqtorate/Legends|Ubiqtorate]] base on Yaga Minor for a copy of the Caamas Document, and timed the meeting to occur just as his assault commenced, in the hopes that it would prove to D'asima that the Remnant was a serious contender for ultimate controller of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Ultimately, however, the meeting was interrupted by the timely arrival of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], who revealed Disra's shady financial connections, Tierce's [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] heritage, and "Grand Admiral Thrawn's" true identity, destroying the alliance before it even began.

Seeing how close she had come to allying the Eleven, and the Mistryl, with a con man, D'asima reluctantly acknowledged [[Shada D'ukal]]'s role in averting the disaster, and agreed to lift the death warrant issued against her, although Shada's exile from [[Emberlene]] remained permanent.

==Appearances==
*''[[Vision of the Future]]'' {{Ab|Vision of the Future (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}}

*<!-- 2008-12-09 -->''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''
